title: Narcissus capax
taxonomy: Narcissus capax is classified within the genus Narcissus, which is a group of flowering plants in the family Amaryllidaceae. This classification places Narcissus capax in the order Asparagales. Within the family Amaryllidaceae, Narcissus capax is further grouped into the subfamily Amaryllidoideae. Narcissus capax is native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. The exact classification of Narcissus capax may be subject to ongoing research and revision.

intro:
There is no information available on "Narcissus capax". It is possible that this is a misspelling or a non-existent plant species.

However, I can provide information on Narcissus pseudonarcissus, also known as the wild daffodil or Narcissus. 

Narcissus pseudonarcissus is a perennial plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, commonly known as the daffodil or jonquil.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is also believed to have a role in reducing anxiety and stress, and may have a potential in the treatment of certain diseases due to its bioactive constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins.

The bioactive constituents of Narcissus pseudonarcissus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Some of the most common alkaloids found in the plant are galantamine, lycorine, and narciclasine.

The most common herbal preparations made with Narcissus pseudonarcissus include infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and memory loss.
